commit.message:
fix: Simplify vercel.json to use single build command

The @vercel/static-build builder was causing deployment issues with Vite.

New approach:
1. Single buildCommand: 'npm run build' (uses root package.json)
2. Single @vercel/node builder for server/server.js
3. Root package.json script handles both client and server builds
4. Routes point directly to client/dist for static files
5. API routes still go to server/server.js

This is simpler and more reliable than the dual-builder approach.

commit.message:
fix: correct model_id on failure + add feature timeout (F05, F13)

F05: Model runner was hardcoding model_id to 'claude' for ALL failed
evaluations regardless of which model failed. Now uses the index from
Promise.allSettled to map to the correct model ID. Fixes corrupted
drift detection and per-model accuracy tracking.

F13: Feature computation Promise.all had no timeout — if Yahoo hangs,
the entire evaluation hangs indefinitely. Now wrapped with withTimeout
at 15s using the existing retry.ts utility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Include client/dist in git and fix gitignore

The root .gitignore was ignoring all 'dist' directories, which prevented
client/dist from being tracked in git. Vercel was building from source,
but the build might have been failing.

Solution:
1. Change gitignore to only ignore server/dist (not client/dist)
2. Force-add client/dist to git repo
3. This ensures Vercel has the built client artifacts

Changes:
- Updated .gitignore to: /server/dist instead of dist
- Added client/dist/ to git repo
- This provides a fallback if Vercel build fails

commit.message:
fix: Remove conflicting server/vercel.json

The server/vercel.json was routing ALL requests (/) to server.js,
which overrode the root vercel.json configuration that properly
separates API (/api/* → Node.js) from frontend (/* → React).

This caused 'DEPLOYMENT_NOT_FOUND' errors because Vercel couldn't
properly route requests.

Solution: Use only root vercel.json which has:
- @vercel/node for server/server.js (API endpoints)
- @vercel/static-build for client (React frontend)
- Proper routing: /api/* → server, /* → client/dist

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add cluster orchestration and classifier

Introduce multi-agent cluster orchestration and task classification features. Adds a TaskClassifier with niyanta integration for complexity/type classification and model recommendations, a CheckpointManager for persisting cluster state, isolation contexts (none/worktree/docker) for safe execution, and a ClusterOrchestrator entrypoint. Update provider sendMessage signatures to accept signal/options, add workspace dependency on @takumi/bridge and @yugenlab/chitragupta, and bump Biome schema; also include VSCode MCP/settings files for local development.

* feat: Complete arXiv multi-agent integration - 6 strategies + bandit learning + 2053 tests passing

* fix: TUI typing lag + add /provider command

- EditorPanel.onInputChange: slash/model/provider completions now sync
  (no async I/O on every keystroke — root cause of unbearable lag)
- @ file completions debounced 150ms (only path that touches readdir)
- CompletionEngine.getCompletionsSync(): new sync-first hot path
- Added PROVIDER_MODELS map + KNOWN_PROVIDERS for all 9 providers
- /provider command: list, switch, hot-swap provider at runtime
- /model command: shows available models for current provider
- AgentRunner.setSendMessageFn(): runtime provider hot-swap support
- AppState.provider signal tracks active provider
- TakumiAppOptions.providerFactory wired in bin/takumi.ts

* Add CLI subcommands, PR automation & Chitragupta

Introduce several features and UX improvements:

- CLI: add subcommands (list, status, logs, export, delete), flags (--pr, --ship, -d/--detach, --issue), issue prefetch via gh, background detach/fork support, and subcommand dispatch in bin/takumi.ts. One-shot prompts now include fetched issue context and provider runs receive autoPr/autoShip options. Helper utilities (formatAge, fetchIssueContext, subcommand handlers) added.

- PR automation: wire auto-create (--pr) and auto-merge (--ship) flows into CodingAgent (creates PR via gh CLI and optionally merges), and plumb options through TakumiApp and agent construction.

- Chitragupta bridge: add VasanaTendency and ChitraguptaHealth types and new API methods (vasanaTendencies, healthStatus) with corresponding unit tests to exercise parameters and malformed responses.

- TUI & UX: add ValidationResultsDialog (new dialog type) and integrate validation flow to surface REJECTs (open dialog, actions to retry/revalidate/view file). Load vasana tendencies and health from Chitragupta on connect and schedule periodic refresh. Update dialog stack handling to push/pop dialogs, add keybindings (command palette alias, model picker, cluster status toggle), provide /validate command to re-run validation, and pass autoPr/autoShip into CodingAgent instances.

- Misc: change agent-runner permission dialog to use pushDialog; add preparatory scripts and tooling: .husky/pre-commit, lint-staged integration and helper _patch_pkg.js; add takumi.code-workspace.

Overall these changes add management subcommands, background and GitHub integration, better observability from Chitragupta, and improved validation UX.

* feat: phase 15 — vidhi, consolidation, fact extraction (#16)

Phase 15 unlocks 3 high-value chitragupta daemon features:

1. **Vidhi system** — learned procedures/rules extracted from past sessions
   - `vidhiList(project, limit)` — list all vidhis for a project
   - `vidhiMatch(project, query)` — match query against learned patterns
   - `/vidhi list` and `/vidhi match <query>` TUI commands

2. **Memory consolidation** — multi-session analysis & compaction
   - `consolidationRun(project, sessionCount)` — extract vidhis, facts, day summaries
   - `/consolidate [count]` TUI command (default: 20 sessions)
   - Generates day files, vidhis, and facts from recent sessions

3. **Fact extraction** — structured knowledge from freetext
   - `factExtract(text, projectPath?)` — extract & save facts to memory DB
   - `/facts <text>` TUI command for interactive extraction
   - Returns typed facts with confidence scores

All methods implement socket-first + MCP fallback pattern.

## Changes

- **packages/bridge/src/chitragupta-types.ts**: +4 new type definitions
  - `VidhiInfo`, `VidhiMatch`, `ConsolidationResult`, `ExtractedFact`

commit.message:
MYTUBE-79 Test: Update video record on success — database fields populated correctly after transcoding (#314)

* MYTUBE-79 test: automate Test: Update video record on success — database fields populated correctly after transcoding

* MYTUBE-79: fix test video duration and Cloud Run job env vars

Three issues prevented the test from passing:

1. Test video source duration was 2 s (color=...d=2) with -shortest flag,
   so -t 10 had no effect — the generated MP4 was still only 2 s.
   The transcoder extracts a thumbnail at offset 5 s; no frame exists,
   ffmpeg exits 0 but writes no file, upload then fails.
   Fixed by setting d=10 in the lavfi color source and removing -shortest.

2. CDN_BASE_URL was not forwarded to the Cloud Run Job via --update-env-vars,
   so the transcoder used the value baked into the job config rather than
   the one the test derives expected_thumbnail_url from.
   Fixed by adding CDN_BASE_URL to the env-var override list.

3. DB_DSN=host=127.0.0.1 was being injected into the Cloud Run Job,
   overriding the INSTANCE_UNIX_SOCKET path already configured on the job.
   The job cannot reach 127.0.0.1 (the local Cloud SQL proxy in the test
   runner), so every DB write failed. Fixed by removing DB_DSN from the
   override list; the job now uses its own INSTANCE_UNIX_SOCKET connection.

Result: 7 / 7 assertions pass in 86.95 s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
